message:
feat(schema): add per-command output-envelope map for agent integration (#265)

Agents driving br as a tool currently have to either hard-code per-command
JSON output shapes (`.[0]` for show, `.issues[]` for list, `.[]` for ready,
…) or inspect output on every call. Surfacing the shape map from the binary
itself removes the ambiguity.

- Add `Commands` variant to `SchemaTarget` that emits a curated map of
  `{shape, jq_filter, items_at, item_schema, error_envelope_on_stderr,
  notes}` per command.
- `br schema all` now bundles both the per-row JSON Schemas and the
  per-command envelope map in one payload (additive; existing per-row
  targets unchanged).
- `CommandShape.item_schema` references the same key namespace as the
  `schemas` map, so consumers can drill from "what command did I run" to
  "what shape are the items I just iterated."
- New invariant test asserts every entry uses one of `array|object|scalar`,
  has a non-empty jq_filter, sets `items_at = "$"` for arrays, and any
  declared `item_schema` actually exists in the schemas catalog.
- Verified empirically against `show`, `list`, `ready`, `blocked`, `stale`,
  `dep tree`, `comments list`, `stats`, `count`, `info`, `label list`,
  `search`, `dep list`.

Closes #265.
